video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 15
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, May 15th: Trump concludes summit with Chinese President Xi; Ship seized off UAE, taken to Iran; Zelenskyy says Russia has fired over 1,500 drones at Ukraine; Plane crashes into home in Akron, Ohio.

video:video.video:title: Affordable art fair in Hong Kong boasts live calligraphy painting and Brit art
video:video.video:description: Live calligraphy from Asia, modern Brit art and ships inside glass whales have all been curated for Hong Kong's Affordable Art Fair.

video:video.video:title: Lady Gaga unleashes 'MAYHEM' in LA
video:video.video:description: On Thursday, Lady Gaga made a dramatic entrance at the premiere of “Lady Gaga: Apple Music Live (MAYHEM Requiem),” a special capturing her one-night-only Los Angeles performance earlier this year that reimagined songs from her Grammy-winning album “Mayhem.”

video:video.video:title: Russia and Ukraine exchange prisoners, with each side releasing 205 detainees
video:video.video:description: Russia’s Defense Ministry said that Russia and Ukraine had a prisoner exchange on Thursday, each side releasing 205.

video:video.video:title: Cannes and the Hôtel Martinez await a 'White Lotus' close-up
video:video.video:description: A look at The Hôtel Martinez, one of the iconic luxury hotels that line the beach in Cannes, which will be a central location for Season 4 of hit TV series, "The White Lotus."

video:video.video:title: US-China summit ends with Xi and Trump claiming progress but differences remain
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump wrapped up his visit to Beijing Friday, flying back to Washington after a a private meeting at Chinese leader Xi Jinping's official residence.

video:video.video:title: Boxing becomes an outlet for girls in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Under the blazing sun in Gaza’s Muwasi area of Khan Younis, girls of different ages line up between rows of tents to practice boxing, throwing punches at worn punching bags and makeshift training pads fashioned from plastic poles and padded tarps.

video:video.video:title: NVIDIA chief Jensen Huang eats noodles on streets of Beijing
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted on Friday trying some of Beijing’s delicacies. He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore, Lucas Bravo among guests at Women in Cinema Gala
video:video.video:description: The Red Sea Film Foundation's annual gala in Cap d'Antibes, South of France, honored Laïla Marrakchi, Genevieve Nnaji, Tara Sutaria, Marie-Clementine Dusabejambo, Kamila Andani and Aixa Kay.

video:video.video:title: Animal welfare group finds systemic neglect at public dog shelters in Romania
video:video.video:description: An undercover investigation into conditions in Romania’s publicly funded shelters for stray dogs, carried out by the international animal welfare organization Vier Pfoten, known as Four Paws, found what it describes as “high death rates and disturbing conditions” amounting to “systemic neglect.”

video:video.video:title: Experts warn motorists not to run down gas tanks too low as prices spike, Memorial Day approaches
video:video.video:description: Experts are warning drivers to be careful not to run down their gas tanks too low as prices continue to spike and the travel-heavy Memorial Day holiday approaches (AP video: Mike Householder

video:video.video:title: India raises fuel prices as global energy crisis puts pressure on economy
video:video.video:description: India raised fuel prices by 3 rupees ($0.03) per liter Friday as the government moved to offset losses due to higher global oil prices.

video:video.video:title: 2 people found dead inside a plane that crashed into an Ohio home
video:video.video:description: Fire officials say two people were found dead in a plane after it crashed into a house in Ohio and exploded into flames.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Steve Cohen ending campaign in Tennessee after redraw of his Memphis district
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Steven Cohen of Tennessee ends his bid for reelection, his career upended by the redistricting battles that are sweeping the country after last month’s Supreme Court decision.

video:video.video:title: Emergency services battle fire following latest attack on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: One person was killed in the latest attack on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia on Friday, according to State Emergency Service of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Jerome Powell's tenure as Fed chair marked by fight for independence while trying to tame inflation
video:video.video:description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell finishes his eight-year term on Friday, leaving behind a complicated legacy as he battled inflation and fought with President Donald Trump to protect the Fed's independence.

video:video.video:title: The lighting technology that brings the Eurovision Song Contest staging alive
video:video.video:description: The countdown is underway to the Eurovision Song Contest grand final. The 70th edition of the event, being held this year in Vienna, is one of the most technically ambitious ever.

video:video.video:title: John Travolta dons beret in Cannes
video:video.video:description: In Cannes, John Travolta walks the carpet with the cast of his directorial debut, "Propeller One-way Night Coach," which is screening in the festival's Cannes Premiere section.

video:video.video:title: Trump said he didn’t ask China's Xi ‘for any favors’ on Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday he did not ask Chinese President Xi Jinping “for any favors” on Iran during their Beijing summit, insisting the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he'll 'be making decisions' on Taiwan arms package after Xi summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he has not yet decided whether a major sale of US arms to Taiwan can move forward following his three-day visit to China.

video:video.video:title: Congressmen on both sides support arms sale to Taiwan despite Trump's hesitation
video:video.video:description: Republican and Democratic member of Congress say they support another arms sale to Taiwan and the U.S. defending the island Democracy, despite U.S.

video:video.video:title: Kyiv mourns as death toll from Russian attack in Ukrainian capital rises to 24
video:video.video:description: The death toll from a Russian missile attack that flattened a Kyiv apartment building rose to 24, officials said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Indonesia's first giant panda cub Rio growing and healthy before his public debut
video:video.video:description: The first giant panda born in Indonesia squirmed as veterinarians checked his hearing and eyesight and confirmed the furry cub was growing and healthy Friday before his public debut later this month.

video:video.video:title: Shakira explains how her 2010 World Cup anthem 'Waka Waka' made her a mother
video:video.video:description: Shakira reminisces about her 2010 FIFA World Cup anthem "Waka Waka (This Time For Africa)," saying the song turned her into a mother. (May 15)

video:video.video:title: Medical plane crashes and sparks fire in New Mexico mountain range
video:video.video:description: A small medical plane crashed in a mountain range outside Ruidoso, New Mexico, before dawn Thursday, killing all four people aboard and sparking a wildfire in the surrounding forest, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment a cuttlefish changes color
video:video.video:description: Underwater footage captures the moment a cuttlefish changed colors from brown to a bright yellow off the coast of Sydney.

video:video.video:title: Paul McCartney and Wings members' artifacts on display at exhibit at Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
video:video.video:description: The largest collection of Paul McCartney's personal artifacts ever publicly displayed is part of a new exhibit at the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in Cleveland, centering on his life after the Beatles and his band Wings.

video:video.video:title: Owl found in concrete mixer is on the mend and maybe a bit wiser from ordeal
video:video.video:description: An adolescent owl that was found stuck in a concrete mixer in southwestern Utah is finally on the mend, flying free and maybe a bit wiser from the ordeal.

video:video.video:title: A political dynasty heiress and a former trade minister advance to Peru’s presidential runoff, final vote tally shows
video:video.video:description: Conservative Keiko Fujimori and progressive Roberto Sánchez will compete for Peru’s presidency in a June 7 runoff election, according to the final vote count from an election held more than a month ago.

video:video.video:title: A mosque and two vehicles are vandalized in the occupied West Bank as settler's violence intensifies
video:video.video:description: A mosque and two vehicles were vandalized in the occupied West Bank village of Jibiya early Friday, according to residents and the Palestinian Ministry of Religious Affairs, as settler's violence intensifies in the Palestinian territory.

video:video.video:title: Trump still mulling US arms sale to Taiwan
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he’s not yet made a determination on whether a major U.S. sale of arms to Taiwan can move forward, following his three-day visit to China.

video:video.video:title: What to know about new Ebola outbreak that has killed scores of people in Congo
video:video.video:description: Africa’s top public health body has confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in Congo's Ituri province. There have been 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths so far.

video:video.video:title: Judge declares a mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s rape retrial after jury deadlocks
video:video.video:description: Jurors deadlocked in Harvey Weinstein’s rape retrial Friday, forcing another mistrial in a #MeToo-era case that has gone to trial three times so far.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano erupts again
video:video.video:description: Kilauea has periodically sent fountains of lava soaring during more than a year of an on-and-off eruption.

video:video.video:title: Maine lumber mill explosion injures at least 11 as firefighters respond to roaring blaze
video:video.video:description: A large fire and explosion at a lumber mill in Maine's midcoast region injured at least 11 people Friday.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike targets Hamas leader in Gaza
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday targeted the leader of Hamas’ military wing, Israeli officials say, but it wasn't immediately clear if Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed or injured.

video:video.video:title: North America’s largest commuter rail system faces a potential shutdown
video:video.video:description: North America’s largest commuter rail system with 250,000 daily riders faces a shutdown if a deal isn't reached with unionized workers to avoid a strike.

video:video.video:title: Israel and Lebanon agree to 45-day extension of ceasefire, US says
video:video.video:description: US officials says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to 45-day extension of ceasefire. It comes after two “productive” days of talks in Washington and will be followed by more negotiations June 2-3, said State Department spokesman.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang eats noodles on the streets of Beijing
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted trying some of Beijing’s delicacies. He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 15
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, May 15th: Trump says he's undecided on sending weapons to Taiwan; Iran's FM says lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the U.S.; Pentagon withdraws U.S. troops from Europe; Judge declares mistrial in Harvey Weinstein's rape retrial.

video:video.video:title: Tennessee man known for posting racist videos gets $1.25 million bond on attempted murder charge
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee man who goes by Chud the Builder and is known for posting racist videos has been given a $1.25 million preliminary bond on attempted murder charges.

video:video.video:title: Researchers unearth Southeast Asia's largest dinosaur
video:video.video:description: A giant new species of dinosaur discovered in Thailand is thought to be the biggest ever found in Southeast Asia.

video:video.video:title: Power outages and fuel shortages deepen frustrations across Cuba
video:video.video:description: Residents in Havana banged pots and pans overnight Thursday into Friday as prolonged power outages and fuel shortages deepened frustrations across Cuba.

video:video.video:title: Maine lumber mill explosion injures at least 11 as firefighters respond to roaring blaze
video:video.video:description: A large fire and explosion at a lumber mill in Maine’s midcoast region has injured at least 11 people.

video:video.video:title: 'Is God Is' writer and director Aleshea Harris on how the film explores unapologetic female rage
video:video.video:description: Award-winning playwright Aleshea Harris is bringing "Is God Is" to the screen. The film follows two twin sisters, Racine and Anaia, who embark on a quest for revenge to confront their dark family history.

video:video.video:title: President Trump returns to White House; says bilateral trip to China was a 'tremendous success'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump called his three-day trip to China a 'tremendous success' after meeting President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Eurovision historian says this year's edition has 'been rather festive'
video:video.video:description: Dean Vuletic, who has been dubbed "Professor Eurovision," discusses protests against Israel’s participation in the annual song contest.

video:video.video:title: A Latino grocery store in Delaware turns its produce aisle into a music stage
video:video.video:description: A family-owned Latino grocery store in Delaware is attracting online audiences and bands from far away with monthly concerts from diverse artists and genres.

video:video.video:title: Lasers and robots: US farmers turning to automation amid labor shortages and aging workforce
video:video.video:description: As the U.S. agricultural workforce shrinks and ages, farmers are increasingly adopting artificial intelligence and robotic systems to manage everything from weed control to dairy production.

video:video.video:title: Beef consumption in Argentina falls to lowest level in 20 years
video:video.video:description: Red meat consumption in Argentina — historically one of the world’s biggest consumers of beef — has fallen to its lowest level in two decades. (AP/ Cristian Kovadloff)

video:video.video:title: Connor Swindells calls Odessa A'zion the 'coolest person I've ever met'
video:video.video:description: Connor Swindells and Odessa A'zion were honored at Chopard’s Cannes party, where Swindells gushed over A’zion, calling her “the coolest person I’ve ever met.” (May 15)

video:video.video:title: Karachi Port emerges as key regional transshipment hub as Strait of Hormuz remains closed
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s ports are unusually receiving bulk cargo shipments due to closure of Strait of Hormuz and reluctance of foreign cargo ships to use gulf ports in wake of ongoing Middle East conflict.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road workers go on strike, halting busiest US commuter rail system
video:video.video:description: Workers on New York’s Long Island Rail Road are on strike, paralyzing the busiest commuter rail system in North America.

video:video.video:title: How to decide when to get a mammogram
video:video.video:description: When should you begin getting mammograms? Guidelines disagree on whether to begin at age 40, 45 or 50, and whether a yearly or every-other-year scan is best.

video:video.video:title: A handful of women who never heard of handball now are training to join the US Olympic team
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of women have tried out for a chance to join the U.S. women’s handball team for the 2028 Olympics.

video:video.video:title: Thai emergency services at scene of crash between cargo train and bus in Bangkok
video:video.video:description: A train crashed into a public bus on Saturday in Thailand’s capital, Bangkok, killing at least eight people.

video:video.video:title: US Africa Command releases video of operation targeting Islamic State in Nigeria
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military released footage Saturday said to show airstrikes in Nigeria targeting the Islamic State group.

video:video.video:title: Delta Goodrem is more than ready for the Eurovision stage
video:video.video:description: Delta Goodrem from Australia will perform the song "Eclipse" in this year's Eurovision Song Contest final in Vienna and says she's 'pumped to hit the big stage.’

video:video.video:title: High-speed passenger hydrofoil boat makes its first seasonal voyage from Sochi to Sukhumi
video:video.video:description: A high-speed passenger vessel "Kometa" made its first voyage of the year on Friday from Russia's Sochi to Sukhumi in Abkhazia, Georgia.

video:video.video:title: It’s been a Greek odyssey for Akylas as he goes for Eurovision glory
video:video.video:description: Eurovision loves an underdog and this year’s surprise success story is a Greek odyssey. From singing on the streets of Athens to representing his country and his generation at the song contest – Akylas is bubbling with excitement about being in the final.

video:video.video:title: 'There is no ceasefire': Displaced people in Beirut unconvinced by extension to Israel-Hezbollah truce
video:video.video:description: Displaced families living in makeshift camps in Beirut remain skeptical about the 45-day extension of the expiring Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire.

video:video.video:title: Finland's contestants explain why they needed to play violin live at Eurovision
video:video.video:description: Finland got special permission for Linda Lampenius to play violin live at Eurovision for her performance with Pete Parkkonen.

video:video.video:title: Uganda confirms case of Ebola in Kampala after new outbreak in neighboring Congo
video:video.video:description: Residents in the Ugandan capital expressed their fears and concerns on Saturday after officials confirmed a case of Ebola following a new outbreak in neighboring Congo.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather in London for rival protests as police separate opposing groups
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of protesters descend on London on Saturday for two major demonstrations.

video:video.video:title: Drones, bullets and cartel warfare fuel an invisible displacement crisis in Mexico
video:video.video:description: María Cabrera and her family fled into the night-cloaked mountains of central Mexico with only the clothes on their backs when bombs fell from the sky and bullets ricocheted off her concrete floors.

video:video.video:title: Two killed in Israeli strike on civilian vehicle in Gaza, health officials say
video:video.video:description: Two people were killed and others wounded after an Israeli airstrike hit a vehicle in Gaza City on Saturday evening, according to local health officials.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 16
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, May 15th: U.S. and Nigerian forces conduct operation targeting Islamic State; Israeli strike kills Hamas military leader; Long Island Rail Road workers go on strike; Humanoid robot monks walk in South Korea parade ahead of Buddha's birthday.

video:video.video:title: Fans gather in Vienna's Volksoper to watch final of Eurovision Song Contest
video:video.video:description: Eurovision Song Contest fans attended a screening of the grand final at the Volksoper, one of Vienna's opera houses. (May 16)

video:video.video:title: Driver crashes into pedestrians in northern Italy, injuring eight people
video:video.video:description: Authorities said four people were critically injured after a car struck pedestrians in the city of Modena, with one woman requiring the amputation of both legs.

video:video.video:title: AP catches up with fans watching Eurovision final at Vienna's Volksoper
video:video.video:description: AP spoke with fans attending a viewing of Eurovision's grand final at Vienna’s Volksoper.

video:video.video:title: Harrelson, Stewart, Le Bon premiere 'Full Phil' at Cannes
video:video.video:description: Woody Harrelson, Kristen Stewart and Charlotte Le Bon walked the red carpet at the 79th Cannes Film Festival. (May 16)

video:video.video:title: North America’s largest commuter rail system shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: The Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, was shut down Saturday after unionized workers went on strike for the first time in three decades.

video:video.video:title: Groups rally to defend Black political representation in Alabama
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people gathered in Montgomery, Alabama, to mobilize support for voting rights amid concerns about states that are moving to dismantle congressional districts that expanded representation opportunities.

video:video.video:title: USS Ford returns home after 11-month deployment supporting the Iran war and Maduro's capture
video:video.video:description: The USS Gerald R. Ford returned home to Virginia on Saturday after an 11-month deployment, the longest since the Vietnam War.

video:video.video:title: Cartel drone attacks empty Mexican towns, force hundreds to flee
video:video.video:description: Alleged members of Los Ardillos attacked communities in Mexico's Guerrero with gunfire, arson and drone explosives, forcing hundreds to flee.

video:video.video:title: Friends and family pay final respects to young woman killed in Russian attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Maryna Homeniuk, an English teacher, and her boyfriend Yurii Orlov, a former hockey player, were killed in this week's Russian strikes on Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: 'Feels like a dream,' Bulgarian singer Dara says after winning Eurovision 2026
video:video.video:description: Bulgarian singer Dara won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday with her infectious party anthem “Bangaranga,” giving the southeast European country its first-ever Eurovision victory.

video:video.video:title: Bulgaria celebrates historic first Eurovision victory as Dara wins with “Bangaranga”
video:video.video:description: Bulgarians celebrated and cheered their first-ever victory at the 70th Eurovision Song Contest with singer Dara’s party anthem “Bangaranga.”

video:video.video:title: Reaction in Taiwan to Trump's remarks on arms sales as bargaining chip for negotiations with China
video:video.video:description: There has been a mixed reaction in Taiwan to U.S. President Donald Trump's remarks on arms sales to the self-ruled island after hearing concerns about it from China's leader Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Eurovision song contest winner speaks about what her song "Bangaranga" means
video:video.video:description: Bulgarian singer Dara won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday with her infectious party anthem “Bangaranga,” giving the southeast European country its first-ever Eurovision victory.
